text.title: spatial-ux-fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the spatial-ux-fundamentals methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every spatial element MUST be placed in exactly one of three field zones: near (0.3-1.0 m), mid (1.0-3.0 m), far (3.0 m+). Mixed-zone placement is forbidden.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG; Meta Quest comfort docs

rule.rationale: Zones derive from human-factors research on reach + vergence + sustained viewing distance for HMDs.

rule.statement: Interactive controls MUST NOT be placed in the far field (>3.0 m). Far field is reserved for ambient context, environmental information, and navigation aids.
rule.rationale: Hand-ray jitter and gaze precision drop sharply beyond 3 m; small controls become inaccessible.

rule.statement: Default content distance for sustained viewing (reading, video, dashboard) MUST be 1.5-2.0 m. Element below 0.4 m or above 3.0 m for sustained viewing requires explicit comfort justification.
rule.rationale: 1.5-2.0 m places content in comfortable vergence range for most users; outliers cause eye fatigue within minutes.

rule.statement: Primary controls MUST be anchored as head-locked-with-decay or hand-attached. Pure world-locked primary controls are forbidden — users drift physically and lose them.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG; Meta Quest UI guidelines

rule.rationale: World-locked primary controls force users to physically walk back to them after movement.

rule.statement: Critical UI MUST NOT be placed in the lower 30° of the vertical FoV (chin region). Repeated downward gaze >30° from horizontal causes neck fatigue within minutes.
rule.rationale: Looking down >30° from horizontal requires neck flexion that fatigues quickly; bottom-edge mobile patterns do not port.

rule.statement: Every app MUST provide a recenter affordance (button + voice command) accessible at all times. Forced-removal of headset is not an acceptable recenter path.
rule.rationale: Users drift, rooms shift; without recenter, primary content becomes inaccessible without removing the headset.
